element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player adjusts atmospheric gases, temperature, and continental drift rates before life introduction
First life forms that can survive outside balanced planetary conditions; foundation for ecosystem

element_introduction_order.element: Multicellular animal taxa (radiates, fish, birds, cetaceans)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Require balanced range conditions; introduce ecosystem complexity and extinction risk
Support food chains; respond to atmospheric conditions; can survive extreme temperatures
Sapient life forms develop cities; introduce technological progression and anthropogenic disasters
Highest technology level; unlocks machine life via nuclear destruction; risk of nuclear winter
Player-controlled interventions with energy costs; direct planetary engineering
Hurricanes, wildfires, volcanoes, earthquakes, tsunamis, diseases, pollution, nuclear war; severity scales with conditions
Followed by evolutionary diversification; catalyst for ecosystem reset and experimentation

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Nanotech Age city destroyed by nuclear explosion

typical_relief_triggers: Civilization advancement to next technological age
Energy budget recovery after infrastructure investment
Mass extinction followed by diversification window
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Open-ended soft pacing with no forced timeline; player controls tempo of evolutionary development across 10 billion year span; tension emerges from systemic imbalances and cascading failures rather than externally imposed sequences

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Machine life as hidden consequence of high-tech civilization destruction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial planetary setup relatively forgiving; single-celled life forms resilient; energy management not critical on lower difficulties
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Ecosystem complexity increases; multicellular life requires balanced conditions; first disaster types emerge; energy budgets tighten
difficulty_curve.late_game: Civilization management introduces anthropogenic disasters; technological progression enables nuclear war and nuclear winter; self-replicating robots threat; planetary death at 10 billion years
notable_spikes: First multicellular life introduction and early mass extinction risk
Transition from natural to civilization-caused disasters
Nanotech Age technological threshold enabling machine life
Earth 2XXX scenario with dual threats (robots and nuclear warfare)

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight scenarios with explicit goals; first three focus on Earth evolution stages (Aquarium, Cambrian, 1990 Earth); middle four involve terraforming other planets (Mars, Venus, Ice Planet, Dune); final scenario is endgame threat response (Earth 2XXX)
Open-ended software toy without required goals; player experiments with ecosystem design and planetary engineering; culminates in either sentient civilization exodus or planetary death
Single-biome implementation of Gaia hypothesis model; daisies adapt color relative to temperature; reduced complexity learning environment

Imbalanced planetary conditions trigger chain reactions: temperature extremes → water state changes (boiling/freezing) → ecosystem collapse → civilization disaster or extinction; no linear progression required
